summary: The case shows a self-reinforcing cycle of low motivation and high anticipatory anxiety that freezes task initiation, with avoidance, negative mood, and self-criticism sustaining inaction. A set of modifiable BIO/PSYCHO/SOCIAL levers spanning activation, anxiety modulation, affect, self-relations, social connectedness, sleep/physical energy, and data-driven tailoring are proposed. Each lever targets specific case criteria (C01–C07), provides a plausible mechanism of change, feasible implementation, and simple metrics to monitor progress over 4–12 weeks, enabling multi-domain synergy to disrupt the avoidance loop and restore action readiness and functioning.

solutions.predictor_variable: Behavioral Activation with structured scheduling and micro-goals
solutions.solution_candidate: Structured Behavioral Activation: implement daily 2–3 short tasks with explicit start times, pre-commitment, and 2–3 minute planning; celebrate small completions to reinforce task engagement.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Activates behavior to counteract avoidance and builds mastery, reducing the anxiety–inaction cycle and increasing self-efficacy.
solutions.measurement: Daily initiated tasks count; time from plan to start; self-rated motivation (0–10); weekly mood
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure tasks are safe and within capability; monitor for overload; adjust as needed.
solutions.mapping_query: Evaluate changes in C01/C03/C04/C05 relative to baseline with weekly task-start metrics.

solutions.solution_candidate: Create specific If-Then plans for initiating tasks and a brief start ritual (e.g., 2-minute routine) to cue action.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Pre-commitment reduces cognitive load and anticipatory anxiety, enabling automatic initiation.
solutions.measurement: Proportion of days with planned task start executed; initiation latency; pre-task anxiety (0–10)
solutions.safety_notes: Plans should be safe and attainable; avoid overload.
solutions.mapping_query: Track adherence to If-Then plans and relation to initiation speed (C03) and anxiety (C02).

solutions.solution_candidate: Weekly 20–30 min mindfulness/defusion practice plus values-driven action planning to reduce reactivity to anxiety.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces experiential avoidance and reactivity to worry, promoting flexible engagement with tasks.
solutions.measurement: State anxiety before tasks (0–10); avoidance episodes per week; practice completion rate
solutions.safety_notes: Approach to distress should be non-triggering; adapt for distress levels.
solutions.mapping_query: Monitor changes in C02 and C04 as mindfulness exposure increases.

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily compassionate self-talk and self-forgiveness exercises to counter self-criticism; journaling of self-supportive responses.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Calibrates negative affect and self-judgment, boosting motivation and resilience to initiate tasks.
solutions.measurement: Self-compassion scale scores; frequency of self-criticism thoughts; daily mood
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain respectful self-relations; avoid harsh self-denigration.
solutions.mapping_query: Assess shifts in C06/C07 via self-report and journaling correlations with initiation attempts.

solutions.predictor_variable: Social support and belonging through social prescriptions
solutions.solution_candidate: Identify 1–2 supportive social connections and/or small-group activities; schedule weekly interactions; escalate to community supports if needed.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Social buffering reduces anxiety, provides reinforcement for activity, and enhances motivation via belonging.
solutions.measurement: Number of social interactions/week; perceived support (scale); anxiety before/after social contact
solutions.safety_notes: Set boundaries; protect privacy; monitor for social overload.
solutions.mapping_query: Examine relation between social engagement frequency and changes in initiation and anxiety (C01–C04).

solutions.solution_candidate: Regular sleep-wake schedule, optimize sleep environment, limit evening screen time; brief daytime grounding to reduce arousal.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Better sleep reduces daytime anxiety reactivity and improves cognitive energy for starting tasks.
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality logs; sleep onset latency; morning mood; readiness to initiate tasks
solutions.safety_notes: If sleep problems persist, screen for sleep disorders; avoid unsupervised sedatives.
solutions.mapping_query: Correlate sleep metrics with initiation and mood changes (C01, C02, C06).

solutions.predictor_variable: Brief daily movement to boost energy and neurochemistry
solutions.solution_candidate: Engage in 15-minute brisk walk or equivalent daily; track adherence; incorporate micro-breaks to reduce resting inertia.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Exercise enhances mood and energy, lowers anxiety reactivity, and supports initiation capacity.
solutions.measurement: Daily movement minutes; mood change pre/post activity; initiation latency
solutions.safety_notes: Adjust to medical status; avoid overexertion; consult if chest pain or dizziness occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: Link days with activity to improvements in initiation (C03/C04) and mood (C06).

solutions.predictor_variable: Measurement-based care and feedback with self-monitoring
solutions.solution_candidate: Daily brief self-check-ins capturing motivation, anxiety, and mood; weekly review; iterative plan adjustments; optional wearables/app dashboards.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Quantified feedback enables rapid tailoring, accountability, and reinforcement of progress across domains.
solutions.measurement: Composite daily scores; adherence to logs; changes in C01–C07; engagement metrics
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure data privacy; obtain consent; escalate if distress increases.
solutions.mapping_query: Use daily self-monitoring to map predictor changes to all criteria and track time-to-improvement.
